title: Exceptions, Diagnostics, Exit Status, and Recovery · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent archive team receives a different authorized corpus, platform, language mix, file size, report consumer, terminal width, error policy, and delivery constraint and transfers exceptions, diagnostics, exit status, and recovery into a changed causal error and recovery contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of causal error and recovery contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes.
Write concise actionable diagnostics containing operation and safe context while retaining a debuggable causal chain.
Map success, usage error, input error, analysis error, and output error to documented stable statuses.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Domain Errors
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 2927 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to map expected failures to causal diagnostics, stable nonzero status, atomic output, and recovery guidance; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Domain Errors.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes. For Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Domain Errors, keep Domain Errors connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Recovery Guidance
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-recovery-guidance-6unz9u" that uses case 4694 to map expected failures to causal diagnostics, stable nonzero status, atomic output, and recovery guidance. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-recovery-guidance-6unz9u
# Competency: bookbot-recovery-guidance.
# Case 4694: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def evidence_bookbot_recovery_guidance_6unz9u(expected_failures: int = 2, mapped_failures: int = 2, exit_status: int = 2, partial_output: bool = False, recovery_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_6unz9u = "6unz9u-u9znu6-4694"
    assert evidence_variant_6unz9u
    evidence_axes_6unz9u = (
        "corpus6unz9u",
        "policyu9znu6",
        "revision4694",
        "command6unz9u4694",
        "reportu9znu64694",
        "test46946unz9u",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_6unz9u)) == 6
    if expected_failures < 0 or mapped_failures < 0:
        return False, mapped_failures, "failure-count"
    if mapped_failures != expected_failures:
        return False, mapped_failures, "unmapped-error"
    if exit_status == 0:
        return False, exit_status, "false-success"
    if partial_output or not recovery_named:
        return False, exit_status, "unsafe-recovery"
    assert exit_status > 0
    return True, exit_status, "causal-failure"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

Build deterministic Python evidence to map expected failures to causal diagnostics, stable nonzero status, atomic output, and recovery guidance; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.

For Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Recovery Guidance, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Retrying every file failure is a safe recovery plan.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Recovery Guidance
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 1131 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to map expected failures to causal diagnostics, stable nonzero status, atomic output, and recovery guidance; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

Competency probe: Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.
